activities_projects.title: Ritual and Legitimacy Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand how non-Kshatriyas gained the right to rule
materials: Reference to the Dantidurga case study
steps: Research the Rashtrakuta chief Dantidurga and his overthrow of the Chalukyas
Explain the significance of the 'Hiranya-garbha' (golden womb) ritual
Analyze why religious rituals were necessary for a ruler who was not a 'Kshatriya' by birth
List other examples like the Kadamba Mayurasharman who gave up their traditional professions for the sword
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the link between religious rituals and political legitimacy in medieval India
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity in explaining the social transition from Brahmin/Trader to Warrior (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Prashastis: Fact vs. Fiction
activities_projects.objective: Critically evaluate historical sources written by court poets
steps: Read the exaggerated claims of victories in the Gwalior prashasti
Contrast these with Kalhana’s 12th-century poem about the kings of Kashmir
Identify why Kalhana is considered a more 'modern' historian (he used varied sources and was critical)
Write a short essay on why we cannot take every prashasti at face value
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops a critical approach toward biased historical records
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison between Kalhana and traditional prashasti writers (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Economics of War: Temples as Targets
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the strategic and financial reasons for targeting religious sites
materials: Story of Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni
steps: Locate Ghazni and Somnath on a map
Identify why Sultan Mahmud targeted wealthy temples rather than fortresses
Explain how Chola king Rajendra I treated the temples of defeated rulers
Discuss the concept of 'looting' as a means to fund grand capital cities like Ghazni
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that medieval temples were symbols of political power and centers of great wealth
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical explanation of the link between temple raids and state finance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mapping the Tripartite Struggle
activities_projects.objective: Examine the geography of power in North India
steps: Mark the territories of the Gurjara-Pratiharas, Rashtrakutas, and Palas
Highlight the city of Kanauj in the Ganga valley
Analyze why control over Kanauj was vital for controlling the fertile plains
Write a summary of why this 'Tripartite' conflict lasted for centuries
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the geopolitical importance of the Ganga valley in medieval politics
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of map marking and understanding of the strategic value of Kanauj (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chola Administration: The Sabha System
activities_projects.objective: Explore the democratic elements of Chola village governance
steps: List the eligibility criteria for becoming a member of the 'Sabha' (land ownership, age, knowledge of Vedas)
Explain the 'Lottery System' used to select committee members
Create a flowchart showing the different committees (Irrigation, Gardens, Temples)
Identify the 'Nadu' and its role in performing judicial and tax functions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the highly organized and participatory nature of Chola local government
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct representation of the Sabha structure and selection process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Lost Wax Technique: Chola Bronze Art
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the technical process behind medieval masterpieces
steps: Sequence the steps: Wax model $ightarrow$ Clay coating $ightarrow$ Heating $ightarrow$ Pouring metal $ightarrow$ Breaking the mold
Explain why this method allows for such intricate details in Chola bronzes
Discuss why these images were often used in temple processions
Identify one secular and one religious subject used in Chola bronze art
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Appreciates the intersection of advanced metallurgy and devotional art
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical accuracy in explaining the lost-wax process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Revenue and Taxation in Medieval States
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how kings funded their armies and monuments
materials: Textbook references to the 400 types of taxes (Vetti, Kadamai)
steps: Define 'Vetti' (forced labor) and 'Kadamai' (land revenue)
Identify who paid these taxes (Peasants, Artisans, Traders)
Explain what the collected revenue was used for (building temples, fighting wars)
Compare medieval tax collection with modern income tax in one paragraph
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the economic burden on the producing classes to support the royal lifestyle
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct definition of tax terms and their usage by the state (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chola Agricultural Expansion
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of the state in environmental management
materials: Images of Chola embankments and canals
steps: Explain the geographical advantage of the Kaveri delta for rice cultivation
Research how the Cholas prevented flooding and managed water distribution
Identify the role of the King in planning large-scale irrigation projects
Discuss how the 'Brahmadeya' settlements helped in clearing forests for cultivation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates state support to the technological advancement in agriculture
activities_projects.evaluation: Analysis of the link between irrigation and the prosperity of the Chola empire (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: From Chahamanas to Chauhans
activities_projects.objective: Trace the rise and fall of a major North Indian dynasty
steps: Identify the region controlled by the Chahamanas (Delhi and Ajmer)
Describe the conflict with the Chalukyas of Gujarat and the Gahadavalas
Detail the significance of the First and Second Battles of Tarain (1191 and 1192)
Analyze the impact of Prithviraja III’s defeat on Indian history
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the role of the Chauhans in resisting early Turkish invasions
activities_projects.evaluation: Historical accuracy of dates and the strategic analysis of Tarain (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Role of Al-Biruni
activities_projects.objective: Understand the value of foreign scholarly accounts
steps: Explain why Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni commissioned Al-Biruni to write about India
Note that Al-Biruni consulted Sanskrit scholars for his work
Identify the subjects covered in his book (religion, philosophy, astronomy, customs)
Discuss why his account is an essential source for historians today
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the importance of cross-cultural scholarly work in recording history
activities_projects.evaluation: Understanding of Al-Biruni’s methodology and his book's significance (10 marks)
